name: Barbell Back Squat
instructions: Barbell | Position bar on upper back across traps. Stand with feet shoulder-width apart. | Descend by breaking at hips and knees simultaneously, keeping chest up and core braced. Lower until thighs are at least parallel to floor. Drive through heels to return to standing position.
instructionsEs: Barra | Coloca la barra en la parte superior de la espalda sobre los trapecios. Párate con los pies separados al ancho de hombros. | Desciende flexionando las caderas y rodillas simultáneamente, manteniendo el pecho arriba y el core contraído. Baja hasta que los muslos estén al menos paralelos al suelo. Empuja con los talones para volver a la posición de pie.

name: Barbell Bench Press
instructions: Barbell | Lie flat on bench, feet planted on floor. Grip bar slightly wider than shoulder-width with wrists straight. | Unrack the bar and lower it with control to mid-chest, keeping elbows at 45-degree angle. Pause briefly, then press bar up explosively to lockout, maintaining shoulder blade retraction throughout.
instructionsEs: Barra | Acuéstate en el banco, pies plantados en el suelo. Agarra la barra un poco más ancha que el ancho de hombros con muñecas rectas. | Saca la barra y bájala con control hacia el centro del pecho, manteniendo los codos en ángulo de 45 grados. Pausa brevemente, luego empuja la barra explosivamente hasta bloquearla, manteniendo las escápulas retraídas durante todo el movimiento.

name: Conventional Deadlift
instructions: Barbell | Stand with feet hip-width apart, bar over mid-foot. Hinge at hips and grip bar just outside legs with mixed or double overhand grip. | Keep back flat and chest up. Drive through heels, extending hips and knees simultaneously to pull bar up along shins. Lock out at top by squeezing glutes. Lower bar with control by hinging at hips first.
instructionsEs: Barra | Párate con los pies separados al ancho de caderas, barra sobre la mitad del pie. Flexiona las caderas y agarra la barra justo fuera de las piernas con agarre mixto o doble pronado. | Mantén la espalda plana y el pecho arriba. Empuja con los talones, extendiendo caderas y rodillas simultáneamente para levantar la barra a lo largo de las espinillas. Bloquea arriba apretando los glúteos. Baja la barra con control flexionando primero las caderas.

name: Barbell Overhead Press
instructions: Barbell | Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, bar resting on front shoulders at collarbone height. Grip bar just outside shoulders. | Brace core and glutes. Press bar straight up overhead, moving head slightly back to clear path. Lock out arms at top. Lower bar with control back to starting position.
instructionsEs: Barra | Párate con los pies separados al ancho de hombros, barra descansando sobre los hombros frontales a la altura de la clavícula. Agarra la barra justo fuera de los hombros. | Contrae core y glúteos. Empuja la barra directamente hacia arriba, moviendo ligeramente la cabeza hacia atrás para despejar el camino. Bloquea los brazos arriba. Baja la barra con control a la posición inicial.

name: Barbell Row
instructions: Barbell | Hinge forward at hips with back flat and knees slightly bent. Grip bar with hands just outside shoulder-width. | Pull bar to lower chest/upper abdomen, driving elbows back and squeezing shoulder blades together at top. Keep torso stable throughout. Lower bar with control to full arm extension.
instructionsEs: Barra | Inclínate hacia adelante en las caderas con la espalda plana y rodillas ligeramente flexionadas. Agarra la barra con las manos justo fuera del ancho de hombros. | Tira de la barra hacia el pecho inferior/abdomen superior, llevando los codos hacia atrás y apretando las escápulas juntas arriba. Mantén el torso estable durante todo el movimiento. Baja la barra con control hasta la extensión completa de brazos.

name: Romanian Deadlift
instructions: Barbell | Start standing with bar at hip level, feet hip-width apart. Grip bar with double overhand or mixed grip. | Keeping knees slightly bent and back flat, hinge at hips to lower bar down front of legs until you feel stretch in hamstrings. Drive hips forward to return to standing, squeezing glutes at top.
instructionsEs: Barra | Comienza de pie con la barra a nivel de caderas, pies separados al ancho de caderas. Agarra la barra con agarre doble pronado o mixto. | Manteniendo las rodillas ligeramente flexionadas y la espalda plana, flexiona las caderas para bajar la barra por el frente de las piernas hasta sentir el estiramiento en los isquiotibiales. Empuja las caderas hacia adelante para volver a estar de pie, apretando los glúteos arriba.

name: Front Squat
instructions: Hold barbell on front shoulders. Squat down keeping torso upright. Drive through heels to stand.

name: Sumo Deadlift
instructions: Wide stance, toes pointed out, grip inside legs. Pull bar up keeping back flat, drive through heels.

name: Incline Barbell Bench Press
instructions: Barbell | Set bench to 30-45 degree incline. Lie back with feet flat on floor. Grip bar slightly wider than shoulder-width. | Unrack bar and position over upper chest. Lower bar with control to upper chest. Press bar up explosively to lockout, maintaining tension in upper chest throughout.
instructionsEs: Barra | Ajusta el banco a una inclinación de 30-45 grados. Acuéstate con los pies planos en el suelo. Agarra la barra un poco más ancha que el ancho de hombros. | Saca la barra y posiciónala sobre el pecho superior. Baja la barra con control hacia el pecho superior. Empuja la barra explosivamente hasta bloquearla, manteniendo tensión en el pecho superior durante todo el movimiento.

name: Close-Grip Bench Press
instructions: Grip bar with hands shoulder-width. Lower to lower chest, press up focusing on triceps.

name: Dumbbell Bench Press
instructions: Dumbbell | Lie flat on bench holding dumbbells at chest level with elbows bent at 90 degrees. Feet flat on floor. | Press dumbbells up and slightly together until arms are fully extended above chest. Lower dumbbells with control to starting position, allowing elbows to drop slightly below bench level for full stretch.
instructionsEs: Mancuernas | Acuéstate en el banco sosteniendo mancuernas a nivel del pecho con codos doblados a 90 grados. Pies planos en el suelo. | Empuja las mancuernas hacia arriba y ligeramente juntas hasta que los brazos estén completamente extendidos sobre el pecho. Baja las mancuernas con control a la posición inicial, permitiendo que los codos bajen ligeramente por debajo del nivel del banco para un estiramiento completo.

name: Dumbbell Shoulder Press
instructions: Dumbbell | Sit on bench with back support or stand. Hold dumbbells at shoulder height with elbows bent 90 degrees, palms facing forward. | Press dumbbells straight up overhead until arms are fully extended. Dumbbells should come close together at top but not touch. Lower dumbbells with control back to shoulder height.
instructionsEs: Mancuernas | Siéntate en un banco con respaldo o párate. Sostén las mancuernas a la altura de los hombros con codos doblados 90 grados, palmas hacia adelante. | Empuja las mancuernas directamente hacia arriba sobre la cabeza hasta que los brazos estén completamente extendidos. Las mancuernas deben acercarse arriba pero sin tocarse. Baja las mancuernas con control de vuelta a la altura de los hombros.

name: Dumbbell Row
instructions: Dumbbell | Place one knee and hand on bench for support. Hold dumbbell in opposite hand with arm extended. Keep back flat and parallel to floor. | Pull dumbbell to hip, driving elbow back and squeezing shoulder blade. Keep torso stable. Lower dumbbell with control to full arm extension. Complete all reps before switching sides.
instructionsEs: Mancuerna | Coloca una rodilla y mano en el banco para soporte. Sostén la mancuerna en la mano opuesta con el brazo extendido. Mantén la espalda plana y paralela al suelo. | Tira de la mancuerna hacia la cadera, llevando el codo hacia atrás y apretando la escápula. Mantén el torso estable. Baja la mancuerna con control hasta la extensión completa del brazo. Completa todas las repeticiones antes de cambiar de lado.

name: Goblet Squat
instructions: Dumbbell or Kettlebell | Hold dumbbell or kettlebell vertically at chest height, gripping top end with both hands under one side. Stand with feet slightly wider than shoulder-width, toes slightly out. | Squat down by breaking at hips and knees, keeping chest up and weight at chest. Descend until elbows touch inside of knees. Drive through heels to stand back up.
instructionsEs: Mancuerna o Pesa Rusa | Sostén la mancuerna o pesa rusa verticalmente a la altura del pecho, agarrando el extremo superior con ambas manos bajo un lado. Párate con los pies ligeramente más anchos que el ancho de hombros, dedos ligeramente hacia afuera. | Baja en sentadilla flexionando caderas y rodillas, manteniendo el pecho arriba y el peso en el pecho. Desciende hasta que los codos toquen el interior de las rodillas. Empuja con los talones para volver a pararte.

name: Dumbbell Incline Press
instructions: On incline bench, press dumbbells up to lockout. Lower to chest level.

name: Pull-Up
instructions: Bodyweight | Hang from pull-up bar with arms fully extended, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width, palms facing away. | Pull body up by driving elbows down and back until chin clears the bar. Squeeze shoulder blades together at top. Lower with control to full arm extension.
instructionsEs: Peso Corporal | Cuélgate de la barra con brazos completamente extendidos, manos ligeramente más anchas que el ancho de hombros, palmas hacia afuera. | Tira de tu cuerpo hacia arriba llevando los codos hacia abajo y atrás hasta que el mentón supere la barra. Aprieta las escápulas juntas arriba. Baja con control hasta la extensión completa de brazos.

name: Chin-Up
instructions: Hang from bar, palms facing you. Pull up until chin over bar.

name: Dip
instructions: Bodyweight | Grip parallel bars and support body with arms fully extended. Lean slightly forward. | Lower body by bending elbows until upper arms are parallel to ground or slightly below. Keep elbows close to body. Press back up to starting position by extending arms. More forward lean targets chest, upright position targets triceps.
instructionsEs: Peso Corporal | Agarra las barras paralelas y sostén el cuerpo con brazos completamente extendidos. Inclínate ligeramente hacia adelante. | Baja el cuerpo doblando los codos hasta que los brazos superiores estén paralelos al suelo o ligeramente por debajo. Mantén los codos cerca del cuerpo. Empuja de vuelta a la posición inicial extendiendo los brazos. Más inclinación hacia adelante trabaja el pecho, posición erguida trabaja los tríceps.

name: Push-Up
instructions: Bodyweight | Start in plank position with hands shoulder-width apart directly under shoulders. Body should form straight line from head to heels. | Lower body by bending elbows until chest nearly touches floor, keeping elbows at 45-degree angle. Push through hands to return to starting position, maintaining rigid core throughout.
instructionsEs: Peso Corporal | Comienza en posición de plancha con manos separadas al ancho de hombros directamente debajo de los hombros. El cuerpo debe formar una línea recta desde la cabeza hasta los talones. | Baja el cuerpo doblando los codos hasta que el pecho casi toque el suelo, manteniendo los codos en ángulo de 45 grados. Empuja con las manos para volver a la posición inicial, manteniendo el core rígido durante todo el movimiento.

name: Leg Press
instructions: Leg Press Machine | Sit in machine with back and head against pad. Place feet shoulder-width apart in middle of platform, toes slightly outward. | Release safety handles. Lower platform by bending knees until they reach 90 degrees or slightly below. Press through heels to extend legs back to starting position without locking knees. Keep lower back pressed against pad throughout.
instructionsEs: Máquina de Prensa de Piernas | Siéntate en la máquina con espalda y cabeza contra el respaldo. Coloca los pies separados al ancho de hombros en el centro de la plataforma, dedos ligeramente hacia afuera. | Suelta las manijas de seguridad. Baja la plataforma doblando las rodillas hasta que lleguen a 90 grados o ligeramente por debajo. Empuja con los talones para extender las piernas de vuelta a la posición inicial sin bloquear las rodillas. Mantén la espalda baja presionada contra el respaldo durante todo el movimiento.

name: Lat Pulldown
instructions: Cable Machine | Sit at lat pulldown station with thighs secured under pads. Grip bar with hands wider than shoulder-width, palms facing away. | Pull bar down to upper chest, driving elbows down and back. Squeeze shoulder blades together at bottom. Control bar back up to full arm extension without letting weight stack touch.
instructionsEs: Máquina de Cables | Siéntate en la estación de jalón dorsal con los muslos asegurados bajo las almohadillas. Agarra la barra con las manos más anchas que el ancho de hombros, palmas hacia afuera. | Tira de la barra hacia el pecho superior, llevando los codos hacia abajo y atrás. Aprieta las escápulas juntas abajo. Controla la barra de vuelta hasta la extensión completa de brazos sin dejar que toque el peso.

name: Cable Row
instructions: Cable Machine | Sit at cable row station with feet on footrests, knees slightly bent. Grip handle with both hands, arms fully extended. Sit upright with chest up. | Pull handle to torso, driving elbows straight back and squeezing shoulder blades together. Keep back straight and avoid using momentum. Extend arms forward with control to starting position.
instructionsEs: Máquina de Cables | Siéntate en la estación de remo con cables con pies en los apoyos, rodillas ligeramente flexionadas. Agarra el mango con ambas manos, brazos completamente extendidos. Siéntate erguido con el pecho arriba. | Tira del mango hacia el torso, llevando los codos directamente hacia atrás y apretando las escápulas juntas. Mantén la espalda recta y evita usar impulso. Extiende los brazos hacia adelante con control a la posición inicial.

name: Leg Curl
instructions: Leg Curl Machine | Lie face down or sit (depending on machine type) with pad positioned just above ankles. Grip handles for stability. | Curl legs up by bending knees, bringing heels toward glutes. Squeeze hamstrings at top of movement. Lower weight with control to starting position without letting weight stack touch.
instructionsEs: Máquina de Curl de Piernas | Acuéstate boca abajo o siéntate (dependiendo del tipo de máquina) con la almohadilla posicionada justo arriba de los tobillos. Agarra las manijas para estabilidad. | Flexiona las piernas doblando las rodillas, llevando los talones hacia los glúteos. Aprieta los isquiotibiales arriba del movimiento. Baja el peso con control a la posición inicial sin dejar que toque el peso.

name: Leg Extension
instructions: Leg Extension Machine | Sit in machine with back against pad and pad positioned on lower shins just above ankles. Grip handles on sides. | Extend legs by straightening knees until fully extended. Squeeze quadriceps at top. Lower weight with control back to starting position, stopping just before weight stack touches.
instructionsEs: Máquina de Extensión de Piernas | Siéntate en la máquina con la espalda contra el respaldo y la almohadilla posicionada en las espinillas inferiores justo arriba de los tobillos. Agarra las manijas a los lados. | Extiende las piernas enderezando las rodillas hasta estar completamente extendidas. Aprieta los cuádriceps arriba. Baja el peso con control a la posición inicial, deteniéndote justo antes de que toque el peso.

name: Barbell Curl
instructions: Barbell | Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding barbell with underhand grip (palms up) at hip level. Keep elbows close to torso. | Curl bar up toward shoulders by bending elbows. Keep upper arms stationary. Squeeze biceps at top. Lower bar with control to starting position without swinging or using momentum.
instructionsEs: Barra | Párate con los pies separados al ancho de hombros, sosteniendo la barra con agarre supino (palmas arriba) a nivel de caderas. Mantén los codos cerca del torso. | Flexiona la barra hacia los hombros doblando los codos. Mantén los brazos superiores estacionarios. Aprieta los bíceps arriba. Baja la barra con control a la posición inicial sin balancear o usar impulso.

name: Dumbbell Curl
instructions: Standing or seated, curl dumbbells up to shoulders. Can alternate or do both together.

name: Hammer Curl
instructions: Hold dumbbells with neutral grip. Curl up to shoulders, keeping palms facing each other.

name: Tricep Pushdown
instructions: Stand at cable, push bar down to full extension. Keep elbows tucked at sides.

name: Overhead Tricep Extension
instructions: Hold dumbbell overhead. Lower behind head, extend back up keeping elbows still.

name: Lateral Raise
instructions: Standing, raise dumbbells out to sides until arms parallel to ground. Lower under control.

name: Front Raise
instructions: Standing, raise dumbbells forward until arms parallel to ground.

name: Face Pull
instructions: Pull cable to face, separating hands at end. Focus on rear delts.

name: Chest Fly
instructions: On bench, arc dumbbells down and out to sides, then squeeze back together at top.

name: Cable Fly
instructions: Standing at cable station, pull handles together in front of chest. Squeeze at contraction.

name: Pec Deck
instructions: Seated, bring arm pads together in front of chest. Squeeze at contraction.

name: Calf Raise
instructions: Standing or seated, rise up on toes. Squeeze calves at top, lower for stretch.

name: Barbell Shrug
instructions: Hold bar at arms length. Shrug shoulders straight up, squeeze traps at top.

name: Dumbbell Shrug
instructions: Hold dumbbells at sides. Shrug shoulders up, squeeze traps at top.

name: Preacher Curl
instructions: Arms on preacher pad, curl bar up. Full stretch at bottom, squeeze at top.

name: Concentration Curl
instructions: Seated, elbow braced on inner thigh. Curl dumbbell up, squeeze at top.

name: Skull Crusher
instructions: Lying on bench, lower bar to forehead keeping upper arms vertical. Extend to lockout.

name: Hip Thrust
instructions: Upper back on bench, barbell over hips. Drive hips up, squeeze glutes at top.

name: Bulgarian Split Squat
instructions: Rear foot elevated on bench. Lower down on front leg. Drive through heel to stand.

name: Lunges
instructions: Step forward, lower back knee toward ground. Drive through front heel to return.

name: Good Morning
instructions: Barbell on upper back. Hinge at hips keeping back flat. Return to standing.

name: Cable Crossover
instructions: High cable handles, step forward and pull down and across body. Squeeze chest.

name: T-Bar Row
instructions: Straddle bar, hinge at hips. Pull bar to chest, squeeze shoulder blades.

name: Pendlay Row
instructions: Bar on ground, explosive pull to chest, lower to ground each rep.

name: Power Clean
instructions: Explosive pull from ground, catch on shoulders. Full body power movement.

name: Snatch
instructions: Explosive pull from ground to overhead in one motion. Highly technical Olympic lift.

name: Clean and Jerk
instructions: Clean to shoulders, then jerk overhead. Technical Olympic lift.

name: Box Squat
instructions: Squat down to box, pause seated, then drive up explosively.

name: Pause Squat
instructions: Squat down, pause 2-3 seconds at bottom, drive up. Builds strength out of hole.

name: Pause Bench Press
instructions: Lower bar to chest, pause 2-3 seconds, then press up. Eliminates stretch reflex.

name: Deficit Deadlift
instructions: Stand on platform 1-4 inches high. Increases range of motion, builds strength off floor.

name: Rack Pull
instructions: Bar starts on pins at knee height. Pull to lockout focusing on top portion.

name: Farmer's Walk
instructions: Hold heavy dumbbells at sides. Walk for distance or time. Full body grip strength.

name: Plank
instructions: Hold push-up position on forearms. Keep body straight, engage core.

name: Ab Wheel Rollout
instructions: Knees or feet on ground, roll wheel forward extending body. Pull back with abs.

name: Hanging Leg Raise
instructions: Hang from bar. Raise legs to 90 degrees. Lower under control.

name: Cable Crunch
instructions: Kneel at cable, hold rope behind head. Crunch down, focus on abs contraction.

name: Reverse Hyper
instructions: Lying face down, swing legs up behind you. Builds glutes and lower back recovery.

name: Back Extension
instructions: On hyperextension bench, hinge at hips and lower torso. Raise back up to parallel.

name: GHD Sit-Up
instructions: On GHD machine, lower back past parallel, crunch up. Full range ab exercise.

name: Nordic Curl
instructions: Knees anchored, lower body forward under control. Eccentric hamstring destroyer.

name: Pistol Squat
instructions: Single leg squat, other leg extended forward. Advanced bodyweight leg exercise.

name: Muscle-Up
instructions: Pull-up transitioning into dip. Explosive upper body power move.

name: Handstand Push-Up
instructions: Inverted against wall, press up and down. Advanced shoulder pressing.

name: Seal Row
instructions: Lying face-down on elevated bench, row barbell or dumbbells. Eliminates lower back.

name: Meadows Row
instructions: Standing perpendicular to bar, one hand row with fat grip end. Unilateral back work.

name: Zercher Squat
instructions: Bar in crook of elbows. Squat down, stay upright. Builds core and front-loaded strength.

name: Arnold Press
instructions: Start with dumbbells at shoulders, palms facing you. Rotate and press overhead.

name: Reverse Curl
instructions: Overhand grip on bar. Curl up, focusing on brachialis and forearms.

name: Incline Curl
instructions: On incline bench, arms hanging. Curl dumbbells up, full stretch at bottom.

name: Decline Bench Press
instructions: On decline bench, press bar from lower chest. Targets lower pecs.

name: Floor Press
instructions: Lying on floor, press bar from chest. Limited range builds lockout strength.

name: Inverted Row
instructions: Under bar or rings, pull chest to bar. Bodyweight horizontal pull.

name: Band Pull-Apart
instructions: Hold band at chest level, pull apart stretching band. Rear delts and upper back.

name: Kettlebell Swing
instructions: Hip hinge swing kettlebell to shoulder height. Explosive hip power.

name: Turkish Get-Up
instructions: Complex movement from lying to standing with weight overhead. Full body stability.

name: High Bar Squat
instructions: Setup: Position barbell on traps (above shoulder blades), feet shoulder-width apart, toes slightly out. Execution: Break at knees and hips simultaneously, descend keeping torso upright and knees tracking over toes. Breathe in on descent. Drive through midfoot to return, exhaling at top. Common mistakes: Leaning forward excessively, knees caving inward. Safety: Keep core braced throughout entire movement.

name: Low Bar Squat
instructions: Setup: Rest barbell on rear delts (lower than high bar), hands wide on bar. Setup allows more forward lean. Execution: Push hips back first, then break at knees. Maintain neutral spine, chest up. Inhale at top, brace, descend, exhale on ascent. Common mistakes: Bar rolling up back, wrists bearing load. Safety: Requires excellent shoulder mobilityâ€”don't force the position.

name: Safety Squat Bar Squat
instructions: Setup: Load safety squat bar (SSB) onto upper back using padded yoke. Hands grip handles in front of chest. Execution: SSB forces more upright torso. Descend controlled, drive through full foot. Breathe in before descent, out during ascent. Common mistakes: Leaning too far forward (SSB will pull you). Safety: Easier on shoulders than traditional barbell, great for injury rehab.

name: Anderson Squat
instructions: Setup: Set barbell on pins in power rack at bottom squat position (just below parallel). Position under bar while in hole. Execution: From dead stop, drive explosively upward to lockout. Lower with control back to pins. Pause 1-2 seconds between reps (no bounce). Common mistakes: Not fully settling on pins, using momentum from descent. Safety: Eliminates eccentric loadâ€”excellent for building concentric strength.

name: Hatfield Squat
instructions: Setup: Use safety squat bar (SSB) on back, hands grip rack uprights or handles for support. Execution: Descend into deep squat while using hands for balance only, not to pull yourself up. Legs do all the work. Breathe normally. Common mistakes: Pulling with arms to assist, not going deep enough. Safety: Allows very heavy loads with reduced spinal stress due to hand support.

name: Trap Bar Deadlift
instructions: Setup: Step inside trap bar (hex bar), feet hip-width. Grip handles at sides, hips slightly higher than conventional deadlift. Execution: Drive through floor with legs and extend hips simultaneously. Bar path is straight vertical. Inhale before pull, exhale at lockout. Common mistakes: Jerking bar off floor, hyperextending at top. Safety: More quad-dominant than conventional, easier on lower backâ€”excellent deadlift variation for athletes.

name: Snatch Grip Deadlift
instructions: Setup: Grip bar with very wide grip (hands near collars), hips lower than conventional. Execution: Pull explosively keeping bar close to body. Wider grip increases range of motion significantly. Breathe in before pull, brace hard, exhale at top. Common mistakes: Grip too narrow, bar drifting away from shins. Safety: Builds tremendous upper back strength but requires excellent mobility. Use straps if grip fails before back.

name: Block Pull
instructions: Setup: Elevate barbell 2-6 inches on blocks or mats. Reduces range of motion. Stand with shins nearly vertical at start. Execution: Pull from elevated position focusing on lockout strength. Drive hips through at top. Common mistakes: Starting too low (defeats the purpose), not fully locking out. Safety: Allows heavier loading than floor pullsâ€”excellent for lockout strength and grip training.

name: Paused Deadlift
instructions: Setup: Standard deadlift setup, feet hip-width, conventional or sumo stance. Execution: Pull bar to just below knee, hold for 2-3 second isometric pause, then complete lockout. Maintain perfect position during pause. Breathe before lift, hold breath through pause. Common mistakes: Losing position during pause, rounding back. Safety: Reduces weight 15-20% vs regular deadliftsâ€”pause builds strength at sticking point.

name: Spoto Press
instructions: Setup: Standard bench press setup, arch, leg drive. Execution: Lower bar to 1 inch above chest, pause for 2-3 seconds without touching, then press. Maintain full-body tension during pause. Breathe in on descent, hold through pause, exhale on press. Common mistakes: Touching chest (defeats purpose), losing tightness during pause. Safety: Builds incredible pressing strength without eccentric stress on shoulders.

name: Larsen Press
instructions: Setup: Lie on bench with feet elevated off floor (legs straight in air or resting on bench). No leg drive available. Execution: Press with upper body only. Slower tempo recommended. Common mistakes: Trying to use same weight as regular bench, bouncing bar off chest. Safety: Isolates upper body, reduces stress on lower backâ€”excellent for developing pure pressing strength.

name: Board Press
instructions: Setup: Place 2-4 inch board (or foam pad) on chest. Standard bench setup with spotter. Execution: Lower bar to board, pause briefly, then press. Reduces range of motion to target lockout. Breathe normally. Common mistakes: Bouncing off board, not controlling descent. Safety: Allows overload of lockout phaseâ€”critical for powerlifters. Requires spotter for safety.

name: Reverse Band Bench Press
instructions: Setup: Attach resistance bands to top of power rack, loop around barbell ends. Bands provide most assistance at bottom, less at top. Execution: Press as normal, bands deload bottom position allowing heavier weight. Common mistakes: Using bands that provide too much assistance. Safety: Excellent for overloading lockout and building confidence with supramaximal loads.

name: Hang Power Clean
instructions: Setup: Start with bar at mid-thigh (hang position), hook grip. Execution: Dip slightly by bending knees, then explosively extend hips and jump, shrugging bar upward. Pull yourself under bar into quarter squat, catching on shoulders. Stand. Breathe before each rep. Common mistakes: Pulling with arms too early, not fully extending hips. Safety: Lighter than full clean, excellent for learning proper triple extension. Master form before adding weight.

name: Hang Power Snatch
instructions: Setup: Wide snatch grip, bar at mid-thigh. Execution: Explosive hip extension and jump, pulling bar overhead in one motion. Catch in quarter squat with arms locked out. Stand to complete. Inhale before pull, exhale after catch. Common mistakes: Pressing out instead of catching with locked arms, jumping forward. Safety: Highly technicalâ€”requires coaching. Start with PVC pipe to learn bar path.

name: Clean Pull
instructions: Setup: Standard clean deadlift position, feet hip-width, hook grip. Execution: Pull explosively from floor, extending hips and ankles (triple extension) and shrugging violently. Bar rises to chest height but you don't catch it. Lower under control. Common mistakes: Not completing hip extension, rowing the bar. Safety: Develops explosive pulling power without technical demand of full clean. Can handle heavier loads.

name: Snatch Pull
instructions: Setup: Wide snatch grip, deadlift position. Execution: Explosive pull from floor with full triple extension and violent shrug. Bar rises to chest/shoulder height. No catch. Lower controlled. Breathe in before pull. Common mistakes: Not keeping bar close, incomplete hip extension. Safety: Builds snatch-specific pulling strength at 100-110% of snatch max. Use straps to focus on power, not grip.

name: Push Jerk
instructions: Setup: Bar in front rack position on shoulders. Feet hip-width. Execution: Dip 3-4 inches by bending knees, then drive explosively upward while pressing bar overhead. Split or jump feet out slightly as you drop under bar. Lock out overhead. Return feet to center, lower bar. Common mistakes: Dipping too deep, pressing before drive completes. Safety: Learn with empty barâ€”timing is critical. Easier than split jerk for beginners.

name: Split Jerk
instructions: Setup: Bar in front rack. Feet hip-width. Execution: Dip and drive explosively, then split legs into lunge position (front and back) while pressing bar overhead. Front shin vertical, back leg slightly bent. Recover by stepping front foot back, then back foot forward. Common mistakes: Not splitting aggressively enough, landing too narrow. Safety: Most technical jerk variation. Allows heaviest overhead loads. Requires ankle and hip mobility.

name: Power Snatch
instructions: Setup: Wide snatch grip, feet hip-width, bar over midfoot. Execution: Pull explosively from floor, extend hips fully, shrug and pull yourself under bar. Catch overhead in quarter squat (no full squat). Stand to complete. Breathe before each lift. Common mistakes: Pulling with arms too early, not locking out overhead in catch. Safety: Less technical than full snatch. Build to 80-85% of full snatch max.

name: Cable Lateral Raise
instructions: Setup: D-handle on low cable, stand sideways to machine. Execution: Raise arm to shoulder height leading with elbow, slight bend in elbow. Control back down. Exhale on raise. Common mistakes: Using too much weight, swinging torso. Constant tension from cable is superior to dumbbells for hypertrophy.

name: Cable Rear Delt Fly
instructions: Setup: D-handles on cables set at shoulder height. Cross cables (left hand grabs right cable). Execution: Pull handles out and back in fly motion, leading with elbows. Squeeze shoulder blades. Exhale during pull. Common mistakes: Using too much weight, pulling with arms instead of rear delts. Keep slight elbow bend throughout.

name: Cable Chest Press
instructions: Setup: D-handles at chest height, staggered stance for stability. Execution: Press forward from chest, full arm extension, squeeze pecs at peak. Return controlled. Breathe out on press. Common mistakes: Standing too close to machine, not maintaining stable base. Constant tension throughout ROM.

name: Cable Tricep Extension (Rope)
instructions: Setup: Rope attachment on high cable, elbows tucked at sides. Execution: Extend arms downward, split rope at bottom to maximize tricep contraction. Return controlled. Exhale on extension. Common mistakes: Elbows flaring out, using momentum from torso. Keep upper arms stationary.

name: Cable Bicep Curl
instructions: Setup: Straight bar or EZ bar on low cable. Stand with slight lean back for tension. Execution: Curl to shoulder height, squeeze biceps, lower controlled. Elbows stay stationary at sides. Exhale on curl. Common mistakes: Swinging, moving elbows forward. Constant tension is the benefit vs free weights.

name: Cable Wood Chop
instructions: Setup: Cable at high position, stand sideways. Both hands grip handle. Execution: Rotate torso while pulling cable diagonally down across body (high to opposite low). Return controlled. Exhale during chop. Common mistakes: Using arms instead of core rotation, not rotating through hips. Anti-rotation core exercise.

name: Cable Pull-Through
instructions: Setup: Rope attachment on low cable, face away from machine, rope between legs. Execution: Hinge at hips, pushing them back while keeping slight knee bend. Drive hips forward explosively to lockout. Breathe in during hinge, out on hip drive. Common mistakes: Squatting instead of hinging, rounding lower back. Perfect for learning hip hinge pattern.

name: Single Arm Cable Row
instructions: Setup: D-handle at chest height, staggered stance. Execution: Pull handle to ribs, driving elbow back. Avoid rotating torso. Squeeze shoulder blade back. Exhale on pull. Common mistakes: Rotating torso excessively, shrugging shoulder. Keep core braced to resist rotationâ€”anti-rotation benefit.

name: Cable Upright Row
instructions: Setup: Straight bar on low cable, shoulder-width grip. Execution: Pull bar to chin height, elbows drive up and out. Lower controlled. Exhale on pull. Common mistakes: Pulling too high (shoulder impingement risk), using momentum. Stop at chin levelâ€”higher can stress shoulders.

name: Pallof Press
instructions: Setup: Cable at chest height, stand sideways to machine, both hands grip handle at chest. Execution: Press arms straight out from chest while resisting rotation toward machine. Hold 2-3 seconds, return to chest. Breathe normally. Common mistakes: Allowing torso to rotate, not maintaining rigid core. Pure anti-rotation core exercise.

name: Kettlebell Clean
instructions: Setup: KB between feet, hip-width stance. Execution: Hike KB back between legs, then explosively extend hips driving KB upward. Guide KB to rack position (resting on forearm against chest) in one fluid motion. Avoid banging wrist. Common mistakes: Curling KB (use momentum, not biceps), death-gripping handle. Let KB float, guide it to rack.

name: Kettlebell Snatch
instructions: Setup: KB between feet. Execution: Explosive hip extension drives KB overhead in one motion. Punch hand through handle at top so KB rests on back of forearm. Lock out overhead. Lower by letting KB fall forward, guide back between legs. Breathe in during down-swing, out at lockout. Common mistakes: Pressing KB overhead (should be ballistic), banging forearm. High skill exercise requiring practice.

name: Double Kettlebell Front Squat
instructions: Setup: Clean two KBs to rack position (both resting on forearms). Elbows high, KBs at shoulders. Execution: Squat keeping elbows high and torso upright. Drive through heels to stand. Breathe in on descent, out on ascent. Common mistakes: Elbows dropping (KBs will pull you forward), not going deep enough. Challenges core stability more than barbell.

name: Kettlebell Overhead Press
instructions: Setup: KB in rack position on one shoulder. Execution: Press KB overhead, rotating palm forward at top. Slight lean away from KB. Lock out fully. Lower to rack. Exhale on press. Common mistakes: Not cleaning KB properly first, arching back excessively. Keep core braced to prevent hyperextension.

name: Kettlebell Windmill
instructions: Setup: Press KB overhead with one arm, lock out. Feet wide. Execution: Keeping eyes on KB, hinge at hips and lean toward opposite side. Free hand tracks down leg toward floor. Return to standing while maintaining lockout overhead. Common mistakes: Bending arm overhead, not pushing hips to side. Requires significant shoulder and hip mobility.

name: Double Kettlebell Swing
instructions: Setup: Two KBs between feet, hip-width stance. Execution: Hinge and hike KBs back, then explosively extend hips to swing KBs to shoulder height. Keep arms straight. Let hips do all the work. Breathe in on backswing, sharp exhale (power breath) on hip snap. Common mistakes: Squatting instead of hinging, using arms. This is a hip hinge movement, not a squat or arm raise.

name: Single Arm Kettlebell Row
instructions: Setup: KB on floor, opposite hand and knee on bench for support. Execution: Pull KB to hip, driving elbow back. Avoid rotating torso. Lower controlled. Exhale on pull. Common mistakes: Rotating excessively, shrugging shoulder. Keep back flat and parallel to ground.

name: Dumbbell Floor Press
instructions: Setup: Lie on floor with DBs at chest. Execution: Press DBs overhead, pause at lockout, lower until triceps touch floor (limits ROM). Breathe out on press. Common mistakes: Bouncing elbows off floor. ROM limitation is the pointâ€”builds lockout strength and reduces shoulder stress.

name: Dumbbell Pullover
instructions: Setup: Lie perpendicular on bench (shoulders only), hips below bench level. Hold single DB with both hands above chest. Execution: Lower DB overhead in arc motion, stretch lats and chest. Pull back to start. Inhale on stretch, exhale on pull. Common mistakes: Bending elbows excessively, going too heavy. Keep slight elbow bend throughout.

name: Dumbbell Chest Press (Neutral Grip)
instructions: Setup: Lie on bench, DBs at chest with palms facing each other (neutral). Execution: Press DBs overhead, maintain neutral grip throughout. Lower with control. Breathe out on press. Common mistakes: Rotating to pronated grip. Neutral grip reduces shoulder stressâ€”excellent for shoulder health.

name: Single Arm Dumbbell Press
instructions: Setup: Bench press position, one DB only. Free hand on hip or across body. Execution: Press single DB overhead while resisting torso rotation. Core works anti-rotationally. Lower controlled. Exhale on press. Common mistakes: Allowing torso to twist, using momentum. Keep shoulders square to ceiling.

name: Dumbbell Romanian Deadlift
instructions: Setup: Hold DBs at thighs, feet hip-width, slight knee bend. Execution: Push hips back, lowering DBs along front of legs. Feel hamstring stretch. Drive hips forward to return. Keep back flat. Breathe in on descent, out on ascent. Common mistakes: Rounding back, bending knees too much (becomes a squat). This is a hip hinge.

name: Dumbbell Lateral Raise (Leaning)
instructions: Setup: Hold support with one hand, lean away at 20-30 degrees. DB in working hand. Execution: Raise DB laterally to shoulder height while maintaining lean. Gravity angle makes medial delt work harder. Lower controlled. Exhale on raise. Common mistakes: Not leaning enough, using momentum. The lean is criticalâ€”it changes the resistance curve.

name: Dumbbell Chest-Supported Row
instructions: Setup: Lie prone on incline bench, DBs hanging straight down. Execution: Pull DBs to ribs, driving elbows back. Squeeze shoulder blades. Lower controlled. Exhale on pull. Common mistakes: Using momentum, not fully squeezing at top. Chest support eliminates cheatingâ€”pure back work.

name: Dumbbell Split Squat
instructions: Setup: DBs at sides, stagger stance with rear foot elevated on bench. Execution: Lower rear knee toward floor, front knee tracks over toes. Drive through front heel to return. Breathe in on descent, out on ascent. Common mistakes: Front knee caving in, not going deep enough. Keep torso upright.

name: Dumbbell Step-Up
instructions: Setup: DBs at sides, box or bench at knee height. Execution: Step up with one leg, drive through heel of working leg (don't push off back leg). Stand fully, then lower controlled. Alternate legs or complete all reps one side. Common mistakes: Pushing with back leg, box too high. Working leg should do 100% of the work.

name: Dumbbell Farmer's March
instructions: Setup: Heavy DBs at sides, standing tall. Execution: Walk with normal gait pattern while maintaining perfect posture. Shoulders back, core braced. Don't let DBs tilt you. Breathe normally. Common mistakes: Leaning to one side, shrugging shoulders up. Distance or time-based (30-60 seconds).

name: Smith Machine Bench Press
instructions: Setup: Bench under Smith machine bar, bar path aligns with mid-chest. Execution: Unrack and lower to chest, press to lockout. Fixed bar path removes stabilization demandâ€”can focus on chest contraction. Breathe out on press. Common mistakes: Bar path not aligned with natural press angle. Adjust bench position to match your pressing angle.

name: Smith Machine Squat
instructions: Setup: Bar on upper back, feet slightly forward of bar (fixed bar path allows this). Execution: Descend straight down, knees track over toes. Drive straight up. Breathe in on descent, out on ascent. Common mistakes: Feet too close to bar (can't balance), going too heavy (no spotter needed but still can fail). Fixed path makes this safer for training to failure.

name: Hack Squat
instructions: Setup: Back against pad, shoulders under pads, feet on platform shoulder-width. Execution: Release safeties, descend until knees at 90 degrees or deeper. Drive through midfoot to extend. Breathe in on descent, out on drive. Common mistakes: Heels lifting, knees caving in. Quad-dominant movementâ€”excellent for hypertrophy.

name: Lying Leg Curl
instructions: Setup: Lie prone, pad behind ankles, knees just off edge of bench. Execution: Curl heels toward glutes, squeeze hamstrings at top, lower controlled. Exhale on curl. Common mistakes: Lifting hips off pad, using momentum. Keep hips pressed down throughout.

name: Seated Leg Curl
instructions: Setup: Seated, pad on lower shins, back against pad. Execution: Curl legs downward, squeezing hamstrings. Return controlled. Exhale on curl. Common mistakes: Not achieving full contraction, using too much weight. Different hamstring angle than lying variationâ€”use both for complete development.

name: Chest Press Machine
instructions: Setup: Seat height adjusted so handles align with mid-chest. Back flat against pad. Execution: Press handles forward to lockout, squeeze chest. Return controlled. Breathe out on press. Common mistakes: Incorrect seat height, bouncing weight. Machines allow training to failure safelyâ€”excellent for hypertrophy work.

name: Shoulder Press Machine
instructions: Setup: Seat adjusted so handles start at shoulder height. Back against pad. Execution: Press overhead to lockout, lower controlled. Exhale on press. Common mistakes: Arching back excessively, not achieving full ROM. Fixed path is safer than free weights for high-rep shoulder work.

name: Cable Lat Pulldown (Wide Grip)
instructions: Setup: Wide grip on bar, seated with thighs under pads. Execution: Pull bar to upper chest, driving elbows down and back. Squeeze lats. Return controlled. Exhale on pull. Common mistakes: Pulling behind neck (shoulder impingement risk), leaning back excessively. Pull to front of chest for safety and better lat activation.

name: Cable Lat Pulldown (Close Grip)
instructions: Setup: V-bar or close grip attachment, seated with thighs under pads. Execution: Pull bar to chest, elbows drive straight down. Emphasizes lower lat. Return controlled. Exhale on pull. Common mistakes: Not pulling low enough (should touch upper chest), rounding shoulders forward. Keep chest up throughout.

name: Seated Cable Row (V-Bar)
instructions: Setup: V-bar attachment, seated with feet on platform. Execution: Pull V-bar to lower chest, driving elbows back. Squeeze shoulder blades together. Return controlled. Exhale on pull. Common mistakes: Using lower back to heave weight, not achieving full contraction. Keep torso at 90 degreesâ€”slight lean but no swinging.

name: Leg Press (Narrow Stance)
instructions: Setup: Feet together at center of platform, lower on platform. Execution: Lower weight until knees at 90 degrees or deeper. Drive through midfoot to extend. Breathe in on descent, out on drive. Common mistakes: Locking out knees, letting lower back round. Narrow stance emphasizes quads. Keep back flat against pad.

name: Leg Press (Wide Stance)
instructions: Setup: Feet wide on platform, toes pointed out 30-45 degrees, higher on platform. Execution: Lower until deep knee bend, drive through heels. Breathe in on descent, out on drive. Common mistakes: Not going deep enough, heels lifting. Wide stance emphasizes glutes and adductors.

name: Seated Calf Raise
instructions: Setup: Seated, balls of feet on platform, pad across thighs. Execution: Lower heels below platform for stretch, then drive up onto toes for peak contraction. Pause at top. Exhale on raise. Common mistakes: Bouncing at bottom, not achieving full ROM. Seated targets soleus (bent-knee calf muscle).

name: Standing Calf Raise
instructions: Setup: Shoulders under pads, balls of feet on platform, legs straight. Execution: Lower heels below platform, drive up onto toes. Pause at top for peak contraction. Exhale on raise. Common mistakes: Bending knees (turns into seated variation), not controlling descent. Standing targets gastrocnemius (straight-leg calf muscle).

name: Archer Push-Up
instructions: Setup: Wide hand placement, one arm extended out to side, one bent under chest. Execution: Lower toward bent arm side, extending opposite arm. Push back up. Alternate sides or complete all reps one side. Common mistakes: Not fully extending off arm, sagging hips. Progression toward one-arm push-up.

name: Pseudo Planche Push-Up
instructions: Setup: Hands at hip level (not shoulder level), fingers pointing toward feet, lean forward. Execution: Lower to full push-up while maintaining lean. Push back up. Breathe normally. Common mistakes: Hands too high (reduces difficulty), not leaning enough. Advanced progressionâ€”builds toward planche.

name: L-Sit
instructions: Setup: Seated on floor or parallel bars, hands at sides. Execution: Press down, lifting entire body off ground with legs extended straight forward (L-shape). Hold 10-30 seconds. Breathe shallowly. Common mistakes: Bending legs, rounding back. Requires tremendous core and hip flexor strength. Use bent-knee tuck progression if needed.

name: Dragon Flag
instructions: Setup: Lie on bench, grip bench behind head. Execution: Raise entire body (shoulders to feet) until vertical, then lower with full body rigid. Only upper back touches bench. Breathe in on raise, out on lower. Common mistakes: Bending at hips, dropping too fast. Extremely advanced core exercise. Start with negatives only.

name: Ring Dips
instructions: Setup: Support on gymnastics rings, arms locked, rings close to body. Execution: Lower with control until shoulders below elbows, push back to lockout. Rings try to push apartâ€”fight to keep them together. Common mistakes: Flaring elbows, not going deep enough. Instability makes this much harder than bar dips.

name: Ring Push-Up
instructions: Setup: Hands in rings, rings at push-up height. Execution: Lower to deep push-up while fighting to keep rings stable. Push back up, turning rings out at top for extra activation. Common mistakes: Letting rings wobble excessively, not controlling descent. Instability adds core demand vs floor push-ups.

name: Front Lever
instructions: Setup: Hang from pull-up bar. Execution: Raise legs and body until completely horizontal, face-up position, arms straight. Hold 5-30 seconds. Breathe shallowly. Common mistakes: Bending arms, piking at hips. Extremely advanced pulling exercise. Use tuck progression (knees to chest) first.

name: Back Lever
instructions: Setup: Hang from bar, rotate backward. Execution: Lower body backward until horizontal, face-down position, arms straight. Hold. Common mistakes: Not fully inverting, bending arms. Requires significant shoulder strength and mobility. Easier than front lever but still advanced.

name: Weighted Pull-Up
instructions: Setup: Hang from bar, weight belt or dumbbell between feet/ankles. Execution: Pull chin over bar, lower controlled. Exhale on pull. Common mistakes: Swinging excessively, not achieving full ROM. Progressive overload for pull-upsâ€”superior to high-rep sets for strength.

name: Weighted Dip
instructions: Setup: Support on parallel bars, weight belt with plates. Execution: Lower until shoulders below elbows, push back to lockout. Keep slight forward lean for chest emphasis. Common mistakes: Not going deep enough, flaring elbows too wide. Excellent for building pressing strength and mass.

name: Band Face Pull
instructions: Setup: Band at face height, anchored. Grip with both hands. Execution: Pull band to face, externally rotating shoulders (hands end wider than elbows). Squeeze shoulder blades. Common mistakes: Pulling to chest instead of face, not externally rotating. Critical for shoulder healthâ€”do these often.

name: Band Dislocates
instructions: Setup: Hold band with wide grip in front of body. Execution: Keeping arms straight, rotate band overhead and behind body in arc motion. Return. Breathe normally. Common mistakes: Bending elbows, grip too narrow. Shoulder mobility drillâ€”start with very wide grip, narrow over time as mobility improves.

name: Band Assisted Pull-Up
instructions: Setup: Loop band over bar, place foot/knee in band. Band provides assistance. Execution: Pull chin over bar, band helps at bottom where you're weakest. Lower controlled. Common mistakes: Using too much assistance, not progressing to lighter bands. Goal is to progress to unassisted pull-ups.

name: Band Resisted Push-Up
instructions: Setup: Loop band across upper back, hands pin band to floor. Execution: Push-up with band providing resistance. Band tension increases at lockout. Common mistakes: Band slipping, not achieving full ROM. Progressive overload for push-ups when bodyweight becomes too easy.

name: Band Good Morning
instructions: Setup: Stand on band, loop over neck/upper back. Execution: Hip hinge, pushing hips back while keeping slight knee bend. Feel hamstring stretch. Return by driving hips forward. Common mistakes: Rounding back, bending knees excessively. Teaches hip hinge pattern with minimal equipment.

name: Band Resisted Squat
instructions: Setup: Stand on band, loop over shoulders. Execution: Squat with band providing resistance that increases as you stand. Breathe in on descent, out on ascent. Common mistakes: Band too tight (can't descend), not controlling ascent. Variable resistance makes top half hardest.

name: Spider Curl
instructions: Setup: Lie prone on incline bench, arms hanging straight down, DBs in hands. Execution: Curl DBs to shoulders, keeping upper arms perpendicular to floor. Lower controlled. Exhale on curl. Common mistakes: Swinging, using momentum. Strict bicep isolationâ€”no cheating possible.

name: Overhead Cable Tricep Extension
instructions: Setup: Rope on low cable, face away, step forward, rope overhead behind head. Execution: Extend arms overhead, keeping elbows stationary and pointed up. Return controlled. Exhale on extension. Common mistakes: Elbows flaring out, not achieving full extension. Targets long head of triceps.

name: Close-Grip EZ Bar Curl
instructions: Setup: EZ bar with narrow grip (hands at first inner bend). Execution: Curl bar to shoulders, elbows at sides. Lower controlled. Exhale on curl. Common mistakes: Elbows moving forward, swinging torso. Narrow grip emphasizes outer bicep head.

name: Wide-Grip EZ Bar Curl
instructions: Setup: EZ bar with wide grip (hands at outer bends). Execution: Curl to shoulders, elbows stationary. Lower controlled. Exhale on curl. Common mistakes: Grip too wide (wrist strain), using momentum. Wide grip emphasizes inner bicep head.

name: JM Press
instructions: Setup: Bench press position, close grip on barbell. Execution: Lower bar to throat/chin area by bending elbows (not to chest). Press back up. Hybrid between close-grip bench and skull crusher. Common mistakes: Flaring elbows, hitting face with bar. Excellent tricep mass builder. Use spotter.

name: Tate Press
instructions: Setup: Lie on bench, DBs held above chest with palms facing forward and elbows out. Execution: Lower DBs to sides of chest in arc motion, bringing DB ends together. Press back up. Unique tricep exercise targeting lateral head. Common mistakes: Using too much weight, not controlling path.

name: Zottman Curl
instructions: Setup: DBs at sides, palms forward (supinated). Execution: Curl up with supinated grip, at top rotate to pronated (palms down), lower with pronated grip. Rotate back to supinated at bottom. Exhale on curl. Common mistakes: Not fully rotating, rushing the movement. Trains biceps and forearms simultaneously.

name: Cable Hammer Curl
instructions: Setup: Rope attachment on low cable, neutral grip (palms facing each other). Execution: Curl rope to shoulders, keep elbows at sides. Squeeze at top. Lower controlled. Exhale on curl. Common mistakes: Elbows moving forward, twisting rope. Targets brachialis and brachioradialis.

name: Dead Bug
instructions: Setup: Lie on back, arms straight up, knees at 90 degrees. Execution: Extend opposite arm and leg simultaneously while keeping lower back pressed to floor. Return. Alternate sides. Breathe normally, never hold breath. Common mistakes: Lower back arching off floor, moving too fast. Anti-extension core exercise.

name: Copenhagen Plank
instructions: Setup: Side plank position with top leg elevated on bench, bottom leg hanging in air. Execution: Hold position, lifting bottom leg to meet top leg. 20-30 second holds. Common mistakes: Hips sagging, not engaging adductors. Adductor strengtheningâ€”critical for groin injury prevention.

name: Landmine Rotation
instructions: Setup: Barbell in landmine attachment, hold end with both hands at chest. Execution: Rotate barbell side to side in arc motion, pivoting feet. Core resists and controls rotation. Exhale during rotation. Common mistakes: Using arms instead of core, not rotating through hips. Anti-rotation and rotational power exercise.

name: Turkish Get-Up (Kettlebell)
instructions: Setup: Lie on back, KB in one hand pressed overhead. Execution: Complex multi-step movement to stand while keeping KB overhead: roll to elbow, post up to hand, bridge hips up, sweep leg through, stand. Reverse to return. Common mistakes: Lowering KB, rushing steps. Learn steps slowly with no weight first. Full-body strength and stability exercise.

name: Suitcase Carry
instructions: Setup: Heavy DB in one hand, stand tall. Execution: Walk while resisting lateral flexion (bending toward weight). Keep shoulders level. 30-60 second walks. Breathe normally. Common mistakes: Leaning toward weight, shrugging shoulder up. Anti-lateral flexion core exercise plus grip work.

name: Birddogs
instructions: Setup: Hands and knees position (quadruped). Execution: Extend opposite arm and leg simultaneously while maintaining neutral spine. Hold 3-5 seconds. Return. Alternate sides. Breathe normally. Common mistakes: Rotating hips, hyperextending back. Core stability and anti-rotation exercise.

name: Landmine Press
instructions: Setup: Barbell in landmine attachment, hold end at shoulder. Execution: Press barbell overhead in arc path (not straight vertical). Landmine creates unique pressing angle. Exhale on press. Common mistakes: Not following natural arc, pressing straight up. Easier on shoulders than vertical pressing.

name: Landmine Row
instructions: Setup: Barbell in landmine, straddle bar, hinge at hips. Execution: Row barbell to chest, driving elbow back. Lower controlled. Exhale on pull. Common mistakes: Standing too upright, using lower back to heave weight. Hinge position must be maintained.

name: Overhead Squat
instructions: Setup: Snatch grip, barbell overhead locked out. Feet shoulder-width. Execution: Squat while maintaining barbell directly overhead. Requires extreme mobility and stability. Breathe in on descent, out on ascent. Common mistakes: Bar drifting forward, not having mobility (don't force it). Technical Olympic lift variationâ€”requires coaching.

name: Thruster
instructions: Setup: Barbell in front rack position. Execution: Front squat down, then explosively stand while pressing barbell overhead in one continuous motion. Lower bar to front rack, repeat. Common mistakes: Pausing at top of squat (should be one fluid motion), not using leg drive to help press. MetCon staple exercise.

name: Bayesian Cable Curl
instructions: Setup: Face away from cable machine, D-handle in one hand, step forward for tension. Execution: Arm starts behind body for pre-stretch. Curl forward and up to shoulder. Enhanced stretch on biceps compared to standard curl. Exhale on curl. Common mistakes: Not stepping far enough forward, elbows drifting too far forward. Named after Menno Henselmans' research on lengthened partials.

name: Sissy Squat
instructions: Setup: Hold support with one hand, feet hip-width. Execution: Lean back while bending knees, lowering torso backward. Knees travel far forward over toes. Rise back to start. Common mistakes: Not leaning back enough, heels lifting. Extreme quad isolationâ€”much harder than it looks. Start with shallow ROM.

name: Cable Kickback
instructions: Setup: Ankle strap on low cable, attach to one ankle. Support yourself facing machine. Execution: Extend hip to drive leg back, squeezing glute. Return controlled. Exhale on extension. Common mistakes: Using momentum, arching lower back. Keep core bracedâ€”glute should do all the work.

name: Glute Ham Raise
instructions: Setup: Knees on pad, ankles secured, body at 45 degrees. Execution: Lower torso toward floor by extending at knees, then pull back up using hamstrings. One of the hardest hamstring exercises. Most need to push off floor initially. Common mistakes: Not going through full ROM, hyperextending back. Build up slowlyâ€”causes severe DOMS.

name: Wrist Roller
instructions: Setup: Hold wrist roller device (rod with rope and weight), arms extended forward. Execution: Roll weight up by alternating wrist flexion/extension. Once at top, roll back down. Breathe normally. Common mistakes: Bending elbows, dropping arms. Keep arms parallel to ground entire time. Brutal forearm and grip exercise.

name: Barbell Wrist Curl
instructions: Setup: Seated, forearms on thighs, wrists hanging over knees, palms up. Execution: Curl bar using only wrists, let bar roll to fingertips on eccentric. Exhale on curl. Common mistakes: Using too much weight, not achieving full ROM. Targets wrist flexorsâ€”use light weight and high reps.

name: Reverse Wrist Curl
instructions: Setup: Seated, forearms on thighs, wrists hanging over knees, palms down. Execution: Extend wrists to curl bar up. Lower controlled. Exhale on extension. Common mistakes: Using too much weight, wrists bending backward excessively. Targets wrist extensorsâ€”very light weight needed.

name: Dumbbell Side Bend
instructions: Setup: DB in one hand, stand upright. Execution: Bend laterally toward DB side, then return to upright and continue bending away from DB. Control throughout. Common mistakes: Bending forward instead of pure lateral, using too much weight. Oblique development exerciseâ€”controversial, can make waist wider.

name: Yoke Walk
instructions: Setup: Position under yoke bar (heavy frame on shoulders). Execution: Walk forward with heavy load on upper back. Maintain upright posture, short quick steps. Distance or time-based. Common mistakes: Looking down, taking long strides. Breathing pattern: short inhales/exhales. Strongman stapleâ€”builds total body strength and mental toughness.

name: Sandbag Carry
instructions: Setup: Pick up sandbag to chest/shoulder height or bear hug position. Execution: Walk with sandbag for distance or time. Maintain upright posture. Common mistakes: Rounding back, taking too long strides. Sandbag shiftsâ€”requires constant adjustment and core stability. Excellent real-world strength builder.

name: Tire Flip
instructions: Setup: Squat down, grip bottom of large tire. Execution: Deadlift tire up explosively, when it reaches chest height, switch grip and press it over. Let it fall to opposite side. Reset and repeat. Common mistakes: Rounding back, not using legs. Powerful hip hinge and pressing movement. Requires heavy tire (400+ lbs for trained athletes).

name: Sled Push
instructions: Setup: Hands on sled handles, arms extended, lean into sled. Execution: Drive legs to push sled forward. Maintain forward lean, drive through balls of feet. Powerful leg drive. Breathe in rhythm with steps. Common mistakes: Standing too upright, not driving hard enough. Excellent for leg development without eccentric stressâ€”minimal soreness.

name: Sled Drag
instructions: Setup: Attach rope to sled, hold rope, walk backward pulling sled. Execution: Walk backward with sled in tow, maintaining tension on rope. Take short steps. Common mistakes: Rope going slack, not maintaining steady pace. Posterior chain and conditioning work. Can also drag forward with harness for different stimulus.

name: Feet-Up Bench Press
instructions: Setup: Bench press position but feet elevated on bench (no leg drive). Execution: Press as normal but without ability to use legs. Focuses purely on upper body pressing strength. Exhale on press. Common mistakes: Trying same weight as regular bench, losing stability. Reduce weight 10-15% from regular bench. Similar to Larsen press.

name: Pin Squat
instructions: Setup: Set pins in rack at desired depth (usually parallel or below). Bar rests on pins. Execution: Position under bar while in bottom position, stand up from dead stop, lower back to pins. Pause on pins between reps. Common mistakes: Not fully settling on pins, bouncing. Builds bottom position strengthâ€”excellent for powerlifters.

name: Tempo Squat
instructions: Setup: Standard squat setup. Execution: Descend for 3-5 second count (slow eccentric), pause at bottom for 1-2 seconds, explode up. Tempo listed as 3-2-1-0 (eccentric-bottom pause-concentric-top pause). Common mistakes: Not maintaining tempo, going too heavy. Reduce weight 20-30% vs regular squats. Builds control and time under tension.

name: Tempo Deadlift
instructions: Setup: Conventional or sumo deadlift setup. Execution: Pull at normal speed, but lower for 3-5 second eccentric. Control the descentâ€”don't drop. Reset and repeat. Common mistakes: Dropping the weight, losing position during slow eccentric. Builds eccentric strength and positional awareness. Use 60-70% of max.

name: High-Incline Dumbbell Press
instructions: Setup: Bench at 60-70 degree incline (higher than regular incline), DBs at shoulders. Execution: Press DBs overhead, bringing them together at top. Lower controlled. Exhale on press. Common mistakes: Not setting incline high enough, flaring elbows excessively. Targets upper chest and front delts more than standard incline.

name: One-Arm Barbell Row
instructions: Setup: Load one end of barbell in corner, straddle bar, grab loaded end with one hand. Execution: Row barbell to hip, driving elbow back. Avoid excessive torso rotation. Lower controlled. Exhale on pull. Common mistakes: Using too much torso english, not controlling the weight. Excellent for unilateral back development and anti-rotation core work.

name: Face Pull (Rope)
instructions: Setup: Rope attachment on high cable, grip rope ends. Execution: Pull rope to face, separating rope ends to pass ears. Externally rotate shoulders. Squeeze rear delts and upper back. Common mistakes: Pulling to chest instead of face, not externally rotating. Do these frequentlyâ€”critical for shoulder health and posture. High reps (15-20).

name: Reverse Pec Deck
instructions: Setup: Face machine, chest against pad, arms extended to sides gripping handles. Execution: Pull handles back in reverse fly motion, squeezing shoulder blades together. Return controlled. Exhale during pull. Common mistakes: Using too much weight, not achieving full contraction. Pure rear delt isolation.

name: Cable Y-Raise
instructions: Setup: D-handles on low cables set close together, stand between cables. Execution: Raise arms up and out forming a Y-shape overhead. Targets upper traps and lower trap fibers. Lower controlled. Exhale on raise. Common mistakes: Using too much weight, shrugging instead of raising. Light weight, focus on trap activation.

name: Bradford Press
instructions: Setup: Barbell at front rack position. Execution: Press overhead to just above head (not lockout), lower to behind head, press back up, return to front. Continuous motion alternating front and back. Common mistakes: Locking out (defeats purpose), going too heavy. Builds shoulder endurance and stability. Use light weight.

name: Dumbbell Incline Fly
instructions: Setup: Incline bench 30-45 degrees, DBs held above chest, slight elbow bend. Execution: Lower DBs out to sides in arc motion, feel chest stretch. Bring back together at top. Exhale on concentric. Common mistakes: Bending elbows too much (becomes a press), going too heavy. Light weight, strict form. Upper chest emphasis.

name: Decline Dumbbell Bench Press
instructions: Setup: Decline bench, feet secured, DBs at chest. Execution: Press DBs overhead. Lower controlled. Exhale on press. Common mistakes: Not securing feet properly, letting DBs drift toward head. Targets lower chest specifically. Many consider it optional if doing flat and incline work.

name: Sled Push
instructions: Load sled with appropriate weight. Lean into sled with arms extended, drive through legs in powerful strides. Explosive lower body conditioning and power development.

name: Sled Pull
instructions: Attach rope or harness to sled. Walk backward pulling sled with steady tension. Emphasizes posterior chain and improves conditioning with eccentric loading.

name: Battle Ropes
instructions: Hold rope ends in each hand. Create waves by alternating arm movements or simultaneous slams. Maintain athletic stance, engage core. High-intensity upper body conditioning.

name: Rowing Intervals
instructions: On rowing machine (Concept2 or similar), perform high-intensity intervals. Drive through legs, finish with upper body pull. Full-body conditioning with low impact.

name: Assault Bike
instructions: Push and pull handles while pedaling on air bike. Maintain steady or interval pace. Arms and legs work simultaneously for maximum metabolic demand.

name: Jump Rope
instructions: Rotate rope with wrists, jump with minimal ground contact. Stay on balls of feet. Excellent for footwork, coordination, and cardiovascular conditioning.

name: Box Jumps
instructions: Stand facing box. Explosively jump onto box, landing softly with full foot contact. Step down carefully. Builds explosive power and conditioning.

name: Kettlebell Swings
instructions: Hinge at hips, swing kettlebell between legs. Explosively drive hips forward to swing kettlebell to shoulder height. Powerful hip hinge conditioning work, not a squat.

name: Med Ball Slams
instructions: Raise medicine ball overhead. Forcefully slam ball to ground, catch on bounce. Explosive full-body movement for power and conditioning. Keep core engaged.
